---
title: "About"
---

I'm an undergraduate at the University of Chicago working on mechanistic interpretability.

My current work focuses on how transformer residual streams organize information across time: which directions are transient, which are persistent, and how persistent geometry relates to PCA, attention outputs, and semantic structure.

## Current Focus

- Residual-stream geometry
- Persistent state and context tracking in language models
- Attention-output subspaces and causal follow-up tests
- Sparse-feature interpretations of geometric directions

## Featured Post

[The Residual Stream Has a Geometry of Time](notes/residual-geometry.qmd)

## Contact

For now, the best public reference for my current work is the featured post above.
